variant: compare 2 acne treatment options — include timeframes like 'week 4 — visible change' in the details

request.message: compare 2 acne treatment options — include timeframes like 'week 4 — visible change' in the details

response_body.response: you're asking about acne specifically, so here's the two main routes from your docs: **adapalene/tretinoin retinoid** vs **azelaic acid**. retinoid is the faster driver for cell turnover and clearing, azelaic is gentler and anti-inflammatory if your skin's reactive.  **pick retinoid if** your acne is moderate-to-severe and your barrier is stable. **pick azelaic if** your skin is reactive, sensitive, or you're coming off over-exfoliation. most people on your routine type (oily/acne-prone) run azelaic AM + retinoid PM once barrier is solid.

visual_blocks.title: acne treatment: retinoid vs azelaic acid

options.name: retinoid (0.05% tretinoin)
cons: can't layer with other actives same night

options.name: azelaic acid (start 2–3×/week)
pros: safe to layer with other actives
cons: less effective on deep cystic acne
may need 8–12 weeks for full effect
